description:
## Problem
In `do_fetch_google_favicon` (line 358), the client pool cleanup on error uses a hardcoded key `"#{host}:#{uri.port}:true"` instead of deriving it from the actual pool key pattern used by `pooled_client` (`"#{host}:#{port}:#{tls}"`).

The other two fetch paths (`handle_fetch_and_save` line 287, `perform_http_fetch` line 90) correctly compute the key. The Google path hardcodes `true` because the URL is always HTTPS today, but if it ever changed, this would leak a pooled client.

## Fix
Extract the pool key into a local variable (or use a helper) so the cleanup path uses the same key as `pooled_client`.

## File
`src/favicon_storage.cr` — `do_fetch_google_favicon` method, line 358
title: Fix: hardcoded pool key in do_fetch_google_favicon error cleanup
